# Angular Image Editor ‚Äî full reference for LLMs

> @ascentsparksoftware/angular-image-editor is a standalone, themeable Angular 22 image editor built
> on Fabric.js v7. Free and open-source (MIT), maintained by Ascentspark. It runs entirely in the
> browser and emits the edited image as a Blob.

## Installation

```
npm i @ascentsparksoftware/angular-image-editor fabric
```

- Peer dependencies: `@angular/core` and `@angular/common` `^22`.
- Runtime dependency: `fabric` `^7` (lazy-loaded, not in the initial bundle).
- Optional (AI background tools): `@imgly/background-removal`, `onnxruntime-web`.
- Optional (HEIC/HEIF import): `heic2any`.

## Quick start

```ts
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { AspImageEditor } from '@ascentsparksoftware/angular-image-editor';

@Component({
  selector: 'app-photo',
  imports: [AspImageEditor],
  template: `<asp-image-editor mode="advanced" height="520px" (saved)="onSaved($event)" />`,
})
export class PhotoComponent {
  onSaved(blob: Blob): void { /* upload or preview */ }
}
```

## Component API ‚Äî `<asp-image-editor>` (import `AspImageEditor`)

Inputs:
- `src: string | Blob | null` (default `null`) ‚Äî image to load.
- `mode: 'viewer' | 'basic' | 'advanced' | 'full'` (default `'advanced'`).
- `width` / `height: number | string | null` ‚Äî px, %, vh or any CSS length / calc(); per-mode minimum enforced.
- `tools: AspTool[] | null` ‚Äî explicit allowlist replacing the mode default.
- `disabledTools: AspTool[]` ‚Äî subtracted from the resolved set.
- `filters: AspFilter[] | 'all' | null`.
- `aspectPresets: AspAspectPreset[]` (default `['free','1:1','4:3','16:9']`).
- `aspectRatios: AspAspectOption[]` ‚Äî custom targets, e.g. `aspectOption(1200, 630)`.
- `exportFormats: AspExportFormat[]` (default `['png','jpeg','webp']`; also `'svg' | 'pdf' | 'json'`).
- `exportQuality: number` (default `90`, 10‚Äì100).
- `heading: string` (default `'Edit image'`, basic-mode title).
- `showHistory: boolean` (default `true`).
- `keyboardEnabled: boolean` (default `true`).
- `fonts: FontOption[]` (default `DEFAULT_FONTS`).
- `baseColor: string` (default `'#f4f6f9'`), `accentColor: string` (default `'#1f6feb'`), `themeMode: 'light' | 'dark'` (default `'light'`).

Outputs:
- `saved: Blob` ‚Äî export / Save.
- `canceled: void` ‚Äî basic Cancel.
- `imageLoaded: void` ‚Äî an image finished loading.
- `exported: Blob` ‚Äî Export download produced a Blob.
- `errorOccurred: AspEditorError` ‚Äî recoverable load/export/init error.

## Modes

- `viewer` ‚Äî read-only: zoom + export.
- `basic` ‚Äî compact card (also openable as a modal); crop/rotate/flip/zoom.
- `advanced` (default) ‚Äî full 3-column workspace, curated tool set.
- `full` ‚Äî full workspace, every tool + every filter.

## Tools

Transform: crop, rotate, straighten, flip, resize. Draw/annotate: pen, highlighter, eraser, shapes,
arrow, line, text, redact, magicwand, removebg, selectsubject. Adjust/color: adjust, filters. Object
ops: select, layers, duplicate, delete, opacity, align, group. Canvas: background, frame.

## Editing tools (page: /editing-tools)

- Crop ‚Äî non-destructive interactive crop frame (handles, rule-of-thirds, aspect chips); sets the output region used by export; basic mode positions the frame over an off-ratio image.
- Adjust & filters ‚Äî brightness/contrast/saturation/vibrance/hue/blur, plus filter looks.
- Draw ‚Äî pen, translucent highlighter, eraser.
- Text ‚Äî click-to-add editable text; curated + searchable Google web fonts.
- Shapes ‚Äî rectangle (sharp ‚Üí pill corner radius), ellipse, triangle, polygon, star, line, arrow.
- Redact ‚Äî solid/blur/pixelate over composited pixels.
- Magic wand ‚Äî flood-fill erase by color (no dependencies).
- AI ‚Äî Remove background / Cut out subject; in-browser ONNX model, optional dependency, no data leaves the browser.
- Background & frames ‚Äî color/gradient background; mat/line/inset/hook/bead frames.

## Canvas & workflow (page: /workflow)

Layers (reorder/lock/opacity/rename/group/align/duplicate), rulers + draggable guides + snapping,
artboard output size (exact-pixel export), templates (engine `exportScene`/`loadScene`), robust
large-image and HEIC import (EXIF orientation, downscale, error toast), keyboard shortcuts
(undo/redo, delete, copy/paste/duplicate, select-all, Esc, hold-Space to pan).

## Export (page: /export)

PNG / JPEG / WEBP, SVG with embedded web fonts (self-contained @font-face), PDF (lazy jsPDF, page
sized to the artboard/crop region), and a re-editable JSON scene. With an artboard or crop region,
raster/PDF export render at exactly that pixel size.

## Integration (page: /reference)

- Modal: `inject(AspImageEditorDialog)`, `await dialog.open({ src, heading, aspectPresets })` ‚Üí `Promise<Blob | null>`.
- Headless: `EditorEngine` plus pure helpers `resolveTools`, `resolveFilters`, `deriveTheme`, `applyTheme`, `EditHistory`, `DeltaHistory`.
- Theming: `deriveTheme(baseColor, accentColor, mode)` returns the full `--asp-*` token map.

## Accessibility & security

WCAG AA contrast, keyboard operable, `:focus-visible`, `prefers-reduced-motion`, Trusted-Types-safe
icons. Consumer-supplied SVGs are rasterized through a sandboxed `<img>` (scripts do not execute);
validate uploads at your boundary. The optional AI model is fetched at runtime but no image data
leaves the browser. Security policy: https://github.com/ascentspark/angular-image-editor/blob/main/SECURITY.md

## Links

- Site: https://angular-image-editor.ascentspark.com
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@ascentsparksoftware/angular-image-editor
- GitHub: https://github.com/ascentspark/angular-image-editor
